signals.title: Social Media Minimum Age Act
signals.summary: The Social Media Minimum Age Act establishes a minimum age of 16 for users to create accounts on specified social media platforms and mandates robust age verification mechanisms.
key_provisions: Minimum age of 16 for social media accounts

signals.title: Regulatory Guidance for Social Media Platforms
signals.summary: Issued by the eSafety Commissioner, this guidance assists social media platforms in complying with the Social Media Minimum Age Act by implementing effective age assurance technologies and clear reporting pathways.
key_provisions: Implementation of effective age assurance technologies
Clear reporting pathways for underage account attempts

signals.title: Industry Standards for Tackling Harmful Online Content
signals.summary: These standards mandate technology companies to prevent misuse of their services for distributing harmful content, including child sexual abuse material.
key_provisions: Prevent misuse for distributing harmful content
Applies to AI applications generating explicit content

signals.title: Children's Online Privacy Code
signals.summary: Developed under the Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2024, this code aims to strengthen privacy protections for children online.
key_provisions: Applies to online services accessed by children

signals.title: Legal Proceedings Involving eSafety Commissioner
signals.summary: The Tribunal upheld the eSafety Commissioner's authority to issue Basic Online Safety Expectations notices, reinforcing regulatory oversight.
key_provisions: Authority to issue Basic Online Safety Expectations notices

signals.title: Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
signals.summary: This amendment prohibits individuals under 16 from holding accounts on specified social media platforms and mandates reasonable steps to prevent underage account creation.
key_provisions: Mandates reasonable steps to prevent underage accounts
